task_name: Unknown FLS Task - Appears to be Vessel Cauterization/Burning Exercise

frame_analyses.description: Red spherical object (appendage/tissue model) with three pink/purple vessel-like structures below it, suspended in trainer box. Instructional overlay present.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational content with on-screen text instructions and avatar guidance

frame_analyses.description: Left instrument (appears to be grasper) approaching red object. Right instrument visible in frame. Text: 'Grasp the object'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructional prompt visible; demonstrates initial grasper positioning

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper grasping red object. Right instrument positioned near vessel structures. Text: 'Stretch the vessels until the object becomes brighter'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows tension application to expose target vessels

frame_analyses.description: Right instrument (cautery/burning tool) approaching the stretched vessel bundle. Red object held in tension by left grasper.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cautery tool positioned for selective vessel ablation. Instructional text: 'Burn the small vessels without damaging the larger vessels'

frame_analyses.description: Cautery tool actively burning/applying heat to vessel bundle. Red object maintained in tension. Multiple small vessels appear to be targeted.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Selective cauterization of smaller vessels while preserving larger ones. Demonstrates controlled heat application.

frame_analyses.description: Continued cautery application to separated vessel segments. Left grasper still maintaining tension on red object.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Systematic ablation of smaller vessels progressing. All three original vessels visible but progressively being treated.

frame_analyses.description: Cautery tool addressing final vessel segments. Red object still grasped. Vessel bundle appearance has changed from original three distinct structures.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Near completion of selective vessel cauterization. Careful positioning to avoid major vessel damage.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments withdrawn. Red object released. All vessel structures appear treated/cauterized. Display shows 'Exercise completed'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion confirmed by system. All targeted structures addressed.

confidence_rationale: Video is classified as INSTRUCTIONAL content, not a performance evaluation. On-screen text prompts ('Grasp the object', 'Stretch the vessels...', 'Burn the small vessels...'), narrative guidance via avatar, and explicit 'Exercise completed' system messages indicate this is a tutorial/demonstration video with educational overlays. No timing information provided, no actual trainee performance to score.
cannot_determine: Task-specific identity (FLS task number) - appears to be a custom or non-standard cautery exercise, not one of the five official MISTELS tasks
Actual completion time - video uses instructional pacing, not real-time performance
Performance score - instructional content is not scored per protocol
technique_summary: This is an educational/instructional video demonstrating a selective vessel cauterization technique. The content shows proper grasping, tension application, and controlled thermal ablation of small vessels while preserving larger structures. The system provides real-time guidance and confirms task completion.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper grasper application to tissue model
Application of gentle tension to vessels for exposure
Controlled heat application to small vessels
Tissue handling to avoid major vessel injury
verbal_cues: Stretch the vessels until the object becomes brighter
Burn the small vessels without damaging the larger vessels
common_errors_addressed: Avoiding thermal injury to major/larger vessels by selective targeting
Maintaining appropriate tissue tension for visualization

note: This video does not correspond to any of the five official MISTELS/FLS tasks (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, Intracorporeal Suture). It appears to be a custom training module, possibly from a surgical skills platform. No performance score is computed per protocol.
